Short title and commencement:- (1) These rules may be called the coinage of One Hundred Fifty Rupees and Five Rupees coins to commemorate the occasion of "lsoth Birth Anniversary of Motilal Nehru" Rules, 2012. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. (a) The coins of the following denominations shall be coined at the Mint for issue under the authority of the Central Government to commemorate the occasion of "150 1h Birth Anniversary of Motilal Nehru", namely:- (i) One Hundred Fifty Rupees; (ii) Five Rupees; j -- 3 of 5 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY (PART II-SEC. 3(i)] (b) the coins of the above denominations.. sh~II conform to the following dtmensions, designs and composition, namely:- - -- -- - Denomination Shape and Number of of the coin outside diameter Serrations (1) ( 2 ) (3) - --One Hundred Circular 200 Fifty Rupees 44 millimeters Five Rupees Circular 100 23 millimeters . •· - ·--- - -·- __ __ __,, __ -•-· •- •· - --- · -· ·· ---- DESIGN 1. ONE HUNDRED FIFTY RUPEES OBVERSE Metal Composition (4) Quaternary Alloy Silver-SO% Copper - 40% Nickel - 5% Zinc- 5% Nickel Brass Copper- 75% Zinc- 20% Nickel - 5% The face of the coin shall bear the lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar in the centre with the legend "fk<H-hl ~ " inscribed below, flanked on the left periphery with the word "~" in Devnagri script and on the right periphery flanked with the word "INDIA" in English. It shall also bear the Rupee symbol"~" and denominational value" 150" in International Numerals below the Lion Capitol. REVERSE The face of the coin shall bear the picture of "Motilal Nehru" in the centre, with inscription •~ ~ ~ ~ 150cft ~• in Devnagri script on the upper periphery and " 150 th BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF MOTILAL NEHRU" in English on the lower periphery. The year "2012" shall be inscribed below the picture of Motif al Nehru. 2. FIVE RUPEES OBVERSE The face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar in the centre with the legend "fkllAcl ~ "inscribed below, flanked on the left periphery with the word 11 m 11 in Devnagri script and on the right periphery flanked with the word " INDIA" in English. It shall also bear the Rupee symbol 11~" and denominational value "5" in International Numerals below the Lion Capitol. -- 4 of 5 -- . The y~ar ~"2012" shall be inscribed below the picture of Motilal Nehru. 3. Standard weight and remedy allowed: - The standard weight of the coins of the following denominations, coined to commemorate the 11 150th BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF MOTILAL NEHRU" and the remedy allowed in making of such coins shall be as specified in the following Table, namely: - Denomination of the coin Standard Weight TABLE Remedy Allowed In Composition In Standard Weight - -- -- -- . --- - _ , - ' -- - - ---- ---- -· ...... - -- -- One Hundred Fifty Rupees Five Rupees ,. ,. _ {~ ----+--~· (3) 35 Grams 1/S00 th plus or minus for silver, 6 Grams that is to say the Silver content may vary from 498 to 502 per 1000 (4) 1/l00 th pl~s or minus that is to say, the weight could vary from 34.65 grams to 35.35 grams. 5 1/l00th plus or minus for Copper and Zinc, that is to say, Copper could vary from 74% to 76% and Zinc from 19% to 21% and 1/200 th plus or minus for Nickel that is to say Nickel could vary from 4.5% to 5.5%. 1/401h plus or minus that is to say, the weight could vary from 5.85 grams to 6.15 grams. , l [F.
